title: वेङ्कटेश्वर स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वेङ्कटेश्वर स्तोत्रम् ॥

कमलाकुचचूचुककुङ्कुमतो
नियतारुणिताटलनीलतनो।
कमलायतलोचन लोकपते
विजयी भव वेङ्कटशैलपते॥ १॥

सचतुर्मुखषण्मुखपञ्चमुखे
प्रमुखाखिलदैवतमौलिमणे।
शरणागतवत्सल सारनिधे
परिपालय मां वृषशैलपते॥ २॥

अतिवेलतया तव दुर्विषहै-
रनुवेलकृतैरपराधशतैः।
भरितं त्वरितं वृषशैलपते
परया कृपया परिपाहि हरे॥ ३॥

अधिवेङ्कटशैलमुदारमते-
र्जनताभिमतादिकदानरतात्।
परदेवतया गदितान्निगमैः
कमलादयितान्न परं कलये॥ ४॥

कलवेणुरवावशगोपवधू-
शतकोटिवृतात्स्मरकोटिसमात्।
प्रतिवल्लविकाभिमतात्सुखदात्
वसुदेवसुतान्न परं कलये॥ ५॥

अभिरामगुणाकर दाशरथे
जगदेकधनुर्धर धीरमते।
रघुनायक रामरमेश विभो
वरदो भव देव दयाजलधे॥ ६॥

अविनाशमचिन्त्यमनादिमजं
परमं परमात्मनमब्जभवम्।
वृषशैलपतेरनलं कुलशे-
खरमद्रिपतेर्भज सेव्यभवम्॥ ७॥

विनापुरुषार्थमनर्थमभी-
ष्टगतिं विगतान्यसमस्तसमम्।
शरणं व्रज वेङ्कटशैलपतिं
त्वदनन्यशरण्यमुदारमतिम्॥ ८॥

श्रीवेङ्कटेशपदपङ्कजधूलिरिह
संसारसिन्धुतरणे तरणिः कृपाब्धेः।
नित्यं हृदिस्थितमनन्तमजं पुराणं
श्रीवेङ्कटेशचरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री वेङ्कटेश्वर स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Venkateswara Stotram ||

Kamala-kucha-chuchuka-kunkumato
niyata-arunita-atala-nila-tano.
Kamalayata-lochana Loka-pate
vijayee bhava Venkata-shaila-pate. || 1 ||

Sa-chatur-mukha-shan-mukha-pancha-mukhe
pramukha-akhila-daivata-mauli-mane.
Sharanagata-vatsala sara-nidhe
paripaalaya mam Vrisha-shaila-pate. || 2 ||

Ati-velataya tava durvishahair
anuvela-kritair-aparadha-shataih.
Bharitam tvaritam Vrisha-shaila-pate
paraya kripaya paripahi Hare. || 3 ||

Adhi-Venkata-shailam-udara-mater
janata-abhimatadika-dana-ratat.
Para-devataya gaditaan-nigamaih
Kamala-dayitan-na param kalaye. || 4 ||

Kala-venu-rava-avasha-gopa-vadhu
shata-koti-vritat-smara-koti-samat.
Prati-vallavika-abhimatat-sukhadat
Vasudeva-sutan-na param kalaye. || 5 ||

Abhi-rama-gunakara Dasharathe
jagad-eka-dhanur-dhara dhira-mate.
Raghu-nayaka Rama-Ramesha vibho
varado bhava deva daya-jaladhe. || 6 ||

Avinasham-achintyam-anadim-ajam
param paramatmanam-abja-bhavam.
Vrisha-shaila-pater-analam kula-she-
kharam-adri-pater-bhaja sevya-bhavam. || 7 ||

Vina-purushartham-anartham-abhi-
shta-gatim vigata-anya-samasta-samam.
Sharanam vraja Venkata-shaila-patim
tvad-ananya-sharanyam-udara-matim. || 8 ||

Shri Venkatesha-pada-pankaja-dhulir-iha
samsara-sindhu-tarane taranih kripabdheh.
Nityam hridi-sthitam-anantam-ajam puranam
Shri Venkatesha-charanau sharanam prapadye. || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Venkateswara Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
कमला (लक्ष्मी) के वक्षस्थल के कुंकुम से सदा अरुणित नील शरीर वाले, कमल जैसे विशाल नेत्रों वाले, लोक के स्वामी, हे वेंकटशैलपति! आपकी जय हो॥ १॥

चतुर्मुख ब्रह्मा, षण्मुख कार्तिकेय, पञ्चमुख शिव सहित समस्त देवताओं के मुकुटमणि, शरणागत वत्सल, सारनिधि, हे वृषशैलपति! मेरी रक्षा करो॥ २॥

अत्यंत दुर्विषह, अनेक बार किये गये सैकड़ों अपराधों से भरे हुए मुझे, हे वृषशैलपति! शीघ्र अपनी परम कृपा से रक्षा करो हे हरि॥ ३॥

वेंकट पर्वत पर विराजित उदार बुद्धि वाले, जनता की इच्छाओं को पूर्ण करने में रत, वेदों द्वारा परम देवता कहे गये कमलापति से बढ़कर कोई नहीं है॥ ४॥

मधुर वेणु नाद से वशीभूत गोपियों से घिरे, करोड़ों कामदेव सम सुंदर, प्रत्येक गोपिका के प्रिय, सुखदाता वासुदेव पुत्र से बढ़कर कोई नहीं है॥ ५॥

सुंदर गुणों के खान, दशरथ पुत्र, जगत के एकमात्र धनुर्धर, रघुनायक, रमापति, विभो! हे दयासागर देव! वरदायी बनो॥ ६॥

श्री वेंकटेश के चरण कमलों की धूलि इस संसार सागर को पार कराने में नौका है। नित्य हृदय में स्थित, अनंत, अजन्मा, पुरातन श्री वेंकटेश के चरणों की मैं शरण लेता हूँ॥ ९॥
benefits: Invokes the blessing of the complete grace of lord venkateswara (tirupati balaji)
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires and removes obstacles
Traditionally recited for removal of accumulated sins and past-life karma
Leads to liberation from the ocean of samsara
description: Venkateswara Stotram — वेङ्कटेश्वर स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Traditional Vaishnavite Literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bestow the complete grace of Lord Venkateswara (Tirupati Balaji) as well as grant prosperity, wealth, and success. Additionally, it is known to protect devotees who surrender wholeheartedly. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar), Saturday (Shanivar), and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Vaikuntha Ekadashi and Ekadashi. Comprising 9 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Venkateswara Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Venkateswara Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Prayer to Tirupati Balaji for blessings, wealth, and devotion.

seoFaqs.question: Where does the Venkateswara Stotram fit within the Suprabhatam corpus?
seoFaqs.answer: The complete liturgical corpus chanted at Tirumala each dawn before the opening of the sanctum is called Venkateswara Suprabhatam and consists of four distinct sections totalling seventy verses. First, Suprabhatam proper (29 verses) is the awakening hymn beginning Kausalya supraja Rama. Second, Sri Venkatesha Stotram (11 verses) is the praise hymn beginning Kamala Kucha Chuchuka Kunkumato, which describes the iconography of the deity. Third, Venkatesha Prapatti (16 verses) is the formal surrender prayer in which the devotee takes refuge. Fourth, Mangalasasanam (14 verses) is the closing benediction. The Venkateswara Stotram in stand-alone usage refers to the second section. (Sources: TeluguOne Venkatesa Suprabhatam; ISKCON Bangalore; Stotra Nidhi; Tirumala Tirupati Yatra.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Venkateswara Stotram and when?
seoFaqs.answer: The entire four-section Venkateswara Suprabhatam, including the Venkateswara Stotram of eleven verses, was composed by Sri Prativadi Bhayankaram Annan (often shortened to PB Annan), a fifteenth-century Sri Vaishnava acharya and direct disciple of Manavala Mahamuni in the Visistadvaita lineage. His title Prativadi Bhayankaram literally means terrifier of opponents in debate, an honorific earned through his scriptural arguments. The composition is held to have been first chanted at the Tirumala sanctum during the late 1400s and has been recited daily without interruption ever since by the Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams archakas as the morning Suprabhata-seva. (Sources: ISKCON Bangalore; Stotra Nidhi; TeluguOne.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the iconographic verse Kamala Kucha Chuchuka Kunkumato describe?
seoFaqs.answer: This celebrated opening verse of the Venkateswara Stotram describes Vishnu's chest as smeared with the kumkuma (vermilion) imprinted from the breasts of Lakshmi (Kamala) when she rests against him, and gives the central iconographic image of Venkateswara as the deity whose chest perpetually carries the proof of Lakshmi's intimate presence. The remaining ten verses describe the four arms holding shankha and chakra in the upper hands and varada and kati-hasta gestures in the lower, the lotus eyes, the makara-kundalas, the yellow pitambara, and the Tirumala hill itself as Venkata-shaila. The composition is essentially a verbal darshan of the moolavar (principal idol). (Sources: Greenmesg Venkatesa Suprabhatam; Vaidika Vignanam; Mahavishnuinfo.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Venkateswara Stotram and Govinda Namavali?
seoFaqs.answer: The Govinda Namavali is a separate but liturgically connected text, consisting of approximately one hundred and eight Govinda Govinda chants used during the daily darshan queue at Tirumala. Pilgrims chant Govinda namalu as they ascend the steps and within the queue lines, while the formal Venkateswara Stotram is chanted in the puja-time slots of Suprabhata-seva at dawn and Ekantha-seva at night. Both invoke the same deity but through different ritual modes: the namalu through ecstatic name-repetition for the masses, the stotram through structured Sanskrit verse for archana. Many pilgrims combine them by reciting the stotram before darshan and the namavali during the queue. (Sources: Tirumala Tirupati Yatra; Stotra Nidhi; vnktchari.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the most auspicious time to recite Venkateswara Stotram at home?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional time is Brahmamuhurta (90 minutes before sunrise), aligning with the Suprabhata-seva timing at Tirumala which begins between 2:30 and 3:00 AM in temple practice. For household devotees, recitation between 4:30 and 6:30 AM after bathing and lighting a ghee lamp before a Venkateswara picture is recommended. Saturday is regarded as Venkateswara's day across South Indian tradition, and Saturday recitation is held to multiply the merit. Other key occasions include Vaikuntha Ekadashi, Brahmotsavam (the nine-day festival in Ashvayuja), Anantha Padmanabha Vratam, and the devotee's personal birth-star day each month. (Sources: ISKCON Bangalore; Tirumala Tirupati Yatra; vnktchari.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do devotees report from regular Venkateswara Stotram recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti embedded in the stotram's closing verse and elaborated in PB Annan's commentary promises liquidation of debts (Venkateswara is famously the deity who took a loan from Kubera for his marriage to Padmavati and whom devotees still help repay through hundi offerings), success in business and salaried employment, fulfilment of marriage proposals, blessings of progeny, protection of children's education, removal of obstacles in property transactions, and ultimately moksha through prapatti (complete surrender). The Tirumala temple's hundi continues to receive enormous offerings precisely because of the lived experience of devotees reporting these material and spiritual benefits across centuries. (Sources: vnktchari Venkateswara Stotram; Tirumala Tirupati Yatra; Stotra Nidhi.)
